title: JavaScript Enabling Quiz
description: Test your knowledge of the essential properties and tools for creating robust and cross-browser web experiences with enabled and disabled JS.

questions.question: Which HTML tag is used specifically to show a message to users who have JavaScript disabled in their browser?
Which of these is the most modern and recommended way to build a professional-grade web application in JavaScript?
options: The 'no-js' pattern using CSS and a single line of JS.

questions.question: Which of these is a possible reason a user might have JavaScript disabled in a professional environment?
options: Using an older version of Microsoft Word.

questions.question: What happens if a web application requires JavaScript and it is NOT enabled in the browser?
options: The browser will automatically download JavaScript.
The application will likely NOT function correctly and show only the HTML content.
The site will load much faster.
The browser will automatically translate the JS into CSS.

questions.question: Where are 'JavaScript Enabling' settings usually located in a modern browser (Chrome, Safari)?
options: The browser's 'Privacy and Security' or 'Content Settings' menu.
Setting is not required for modern sites.
